creator: pieter@degoeje.nl
text:
--Boundary-00=_vfACKZVOn48ugxP Content-Type: text/plain; charset="iso-8859-15" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit On Sunday 10 May 2009 23:07:26 Jonathan Chen wrote: > Currently, the java/openjdk6 port will only use diablo-jdk1.6.0 > to boostrap itself. This patch will allow it to use a currently > installed java/openjdk6 port or a java/jdk16 port as bootstrap > jdks in preference to diablo-jdk. It would also be nice to add an entry to bsd.java.mk, something like this: _JAVA_PORT_NATIVE_OPEN_JDK_1_6_INFO= HOME=${LOCALBASE}/openjdk6 \ VERSION=1.6.0 OS=native VENDOR=sun(?) Other ports will then use it as a dependency instead of building diablo-jdk. -- Pieter de Goeje --Boundary-00=_vfACKZVOn48ugxP Content-Type: text/html; charset="iso-8859-15" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit On Sunday 10 May 2009 23:07:26 Jonathan Chen wrote:
> Currently, the java/openjdk6 port will only use diablo-jdk1.6.0
> to boostrap itself. This patch will allow it to use a currently
> installed java/openjdk6 port or a java/jdk16 port as bootstrap
> jdks in preference to diablo-jdk.


It would also be nice to add an entry to bsd.java.mk, something like this:
_JAVA_PORT_NATIVE_OPEN_JDK_1_6_INFO= HOME=${LOCALBASE}/openjdk6 \
VERSION=1.6.0 OS=native VENDOR=sun(?)


Other ports will then use it as a dependency instead of building diablo-jdk.


--
Pieter de Goeje


--Boundary-00=_vfACKZVOn48ugxP--
